Wurm Scale Coat  
 

Lore Item
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 8
SV FIRE: +20 SV MAGIC: +10
Focus: Improved Damage II
WT: 0.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 8
SV FIRE: +20 SV MAGIC: +10
Focus: Improved Damage II
WT: 0.0 Size: MEDIUM

Race: ALL

-466, -2547
▲ Page top
Cool
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2003 at 12:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175426080
23 posts
Got one two nights ago from a guy getting rid of all his items for ridiculously cheap prices. sold this to me for 20pp. when i did a price check i got about 20 replies with everything from 2k to 5k. can't decide whether to keep it or sell it, but if this effects my lifetaps, i think that helps the decision.
▲ Page top
Nice addition
QuoteReply
# May 14 2003 at 10:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362014850
View User Forum
68 posts
Just put one of these on my Wizzy. Picked it up for 2700p in the bazaar on Innoruuk.
My Wizzy is only lvl 16 but has Extended range/spell haste/mana presarvation/improved Dmg, all II's so hes good till lvl 49 spells for nuking!
Personally I think the II's are the best buy in the focus effects. Anything higher and You pay a fortune for it and the I's don't last long cause getting to lvl 30 goes so quick.
Adding all these effects together makes a big difference to a Wizzy I think. Looking forward to dishing out Dmg with this guy.
▲ Page top
2up at 1 time

Wacked 2 at 1 time was soloin 1 and other jumped me lol was fun killin 2 at the samew time lol at 61 i resisted all but 1 spell but it on no coat no sapells just junk >

bought it from a dude in bazaar for 600
QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2003 at 12:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361674561
37 posts
I was looking for s new set of shoulders and saw this for my ranger. Ok ac, NICE resists and I saw that ID2 could help me a lot. Next week I saw it being auctioned for 8k! Don't know if i wanna let go of it hehe.
▲ Page top
procs
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2002 at 12:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361737014
226 posts
does ID2 effect weapon procs?
▲ Page top
RE: procs
QuoteReply
# Dec 19 2002 at 5:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772738273
77 posts
yes. procs are affected by focus items.
▲ Page top
RE: procs
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2003 at 2:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1613953023551
99 posts
More specifically, weapon procs for spells that occur on your character's spell list. My rogue uses a Wakizashi of the Frozen Skies, and had a Mammoth Hide Cloak (ID I) equipped. No effect, despite the fact that the Spirit Strike spell is sub-Lv20.
▲ Page top
RE: procs
QuoteReply
# Dec 27 2003 at 3:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760414965
8 posts
That is incorrect. The reason ID does not affect your procs is your class. Pure melees (warriors, monks and rogues) gain no benefit from any spell focus.

Edit: bards also suffer from this penalty.

Edited, Sat Dec 27 15:24:42 2003
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 15 2002 at 5:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Wow, WAY TO GO VERANT !! another absolutely brilliant masperpiece of common sense, I'mproved Damage 2 "Does NOT effect spells higher than 44", thats a stroke of genius considering youde need a group of at least level 49+ to even kill the mobs that drop it, man o man I cannot wait to get my hands on items with more redundant effects like this
RE: yay verant how erm clever ??
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2002 at 6:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592255076925
View User Forum
172 posts
well... all the focus effect things are for better twinking, isn't it clear? except for the level 3 effects which are for saving allllll your cash up for the good ones

Edited, Sun Aug 18 18:53:40 2002

QuoteReply
# Aug 03 2002 at 6:27 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361853491
1 post
Now it has Improved Damage 2 on it, makes it a Caster piece I guess ;-)
▲ Page top
RE: Effect
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2002 at 7:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758106980
222 posts
Nope - an SK would do rather well with this sucker for his lifetaps, since it not only increases the amount of damage that the lifetaps will do, but the amount of healing that they will do as well.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 28 2002 at 5:09 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) there is no way in hell that this is worth 10k. you can take the froggy crown into perspective. it has 12ac 20cr 20fr one of the monks best peices. if i had a choice between this for 10k and a drolvarg mantle for 175pp and i had a total of 100k on me i would buy the drolvarg. 10k would be a complete waste for this item
RE: i agree with lonaleb
QuoteReply
# Sep 18 2002 at 1:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758849502
5 posts
Resent changes to resists plus the addition of focus effect makes this item more valuable than it was in the past. 10k is not unreasonable. Maybe you don't value the 10+ Magic Resist? I can tell you as a monk that there is nothing worse than being slowed! I try to keep my MR as high as I can and this has one of the highest MR on a tradable item.

Edited, Wed Sep 18 14:17:28 2002
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 02 2002 at 8:11 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) OMFG YOUR MONK IS DSIGUSTING AND OBSCENE good thing it's not real lmao.
Froggy Crown
QuoteReply
# May 18 2002 at 10:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251077466
133 posts
HOW can you compare this to a froggy crown?! Two different slots! Two different zones! Two very very different mobs! Froggy crown is MUCH easier to get - however, these mobs in BW are MUCH harder to kill. 1 wt is a BIG difference for a monk. AND, this has RM 10 insteand of RC 20 -- magic is MUCH harder to get up than cold, thus jacking up the price even more. I would agree - this thing probably does go for 10k.
▲ Page top
Nice. I need one :D
QuoteReply
# May 05 2002 at 8:01 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591772519444
80 posts
Almost won this a month ago, guild was helping do mage epic, those danm named wurms hardly ever drop the mage embers.. took 5 hours the first night we were there, and we got them, but she put them in a no rent bag.. LOL and we also go a wurm scale coat, which I lost the roll on, and we came back the next day for about three hours and got her the embers back, but no coat.. seems like a random drop, danm they drop those guts like mad, how annoying. ez for bards to get that piece of the epic eh? :D

RE: Gylton drop?
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2002 at 3:58 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780918615
35 posts
In regards to Gylton or even Azdalin dropping this, I have killed them both dozens of times and haven't seen it drop yet. On the other hand, Nezekezena is a much tougher wurm to kill. I killed him twice and felt lucky to do so without getting killed myself. I haven't tried Phurzikon yet - the only time I saw him, was right after the first time I killed Nezekezena and I was out of mana.

Ghoste
55 Defilier
Xegony Server

Edited, Wed Nov 20 03:37:28 2002
▲ Page top
Regarding Price
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2001 at 1:59 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591757889512
Sage
72 posts
Almost any monk who is high enough level that he can afford to pay 25k for something will just get a group of his friends together and get this item for free. As a matter of fact, that is what I am planning to do in the near future myself. The only time you would see a monk pay 25k for this would be (A) if they purchased the platinum on E-Bay for their low level monk or (B) if they had obscene amounts of cash and were feeling too lazy to go after it themselves or (C) if they are one of those people that buys and sells stuff all the time in EC and makes 50k by the time they are level 10 (with 100 days played). Twenty five thousand platinum can be spent to much greater effect on other, harder to get items. Personally, I would probably pay about 3000 maximum for this item if I were going to buy it. I am not putting down the item by the way. It is very nice. It just isn't worth 25k.
▲ Page top
Yep, they drop
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2001 at 3:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754291614
7 posts
We killed 2 Nezekezena and 2 Phurzikon last night and were incredibly lucky - 2 coats dropped, both from Phurzikon. On other occasions, we've killed them for hours without any luck.
▲ Page top
Not wandering
QuoteReply
# Jul 12 2001 at 3:01 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246706946
2 posts
Nezekezena and Phurzikon are NOT random spawns. They do how every wander from their static spawn point after a few minutes or so(they never lived long enuff to wander tho :P) The spawn point i will post it tomorrow as i already logged for the night and forgot to write it down. When u first get to its static location tho if nothing is there u pull all surrounding mobs. eventually u will get the PH and at that point every 90 SECONDS one of them(Nez or Phur) or the ph will spawn. PH can be anything from a sarnak to a skeleton to a giant to a regular wurm and so on. We were doin a mage epic and we killed the named spawns over 20 times. Our group consisted of a mage an enchanter for mezzing a druid for snaring a bard for fear a monk and a cleric. We fear/snared everything and cleric didnt have to do a damn thing. So if u cant get a cleric ur fine...just find some to fill the 6th spot and go hack em down! BTW this is great exp. 90 seconds in between fights cant be beat.

Zager 51 monk
Cazic-Thule
▲ Page top
RE: Not wandering
QuoteReply
# Feb 16 2002 at 8:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361153562
20 posts
If this post is true, and it does sound convinceing after seeing how BW works, id really like to know that loc, so please post it, or email me the loc Leshroc@hotmail.com much thanks.

Treehugin
60th Druid
Defiant of Vallon Zek

Edited, Sat Feb 16 20:39:09 2002

Phurzakon drops this
QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2001 at 5:24 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Nez dose not drop this item Phurzakon dose. Nez drops Bard epic guts, Phurzakon drops Mage epic part Burning Embers on very rare and Wurmscale coat on Rare. Both wurms spawn at same location in burning woods

Removed?
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 7:01 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This item is either pretty rare, or has been removed. I've killed this guy 14 times straight, and all he ever dropped were the guts for the bard quest
▲ Page top
drop rate changed?
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 4:44 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Can anyone confirm if Verant changed the drop rate, or even if the wurmscale coat drops at all anymore? I was out hunting specifically for Phurzikon/Nez in BW with a necro and druid. We found a spot that spawned both of them, not the generally accepted loc by the burnt out trees, but another near skyfire. We killed four Phurzikons and three Nezes. All the Phurzs dropped nothing, and the Nezes only dropped guts. Considering people were getting consistent drops even recently, something must've been changed.
▲ Page top
RE: drop rate changed?
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2001 at 1:31 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I can confirm that they still drop. Picked one up this week off of Phurzikon. Nice item...
▲ Page top
Loc
QuoteReply
# May 13 2001 at 1:17 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I wish I saw Nez at L46 heh. Everytime I've seen her she was an even con at 51. We (51 Nec/Mag) tried her, with getting summoned it was a quick fight in her favor. We can kill Gullerback, Gorgul, Mature Wurms in SF, etc., but between summons and nukes the 51 variety is nasty, so if you're not sure she's not 51 be prepared for a lot of pain, hope you live heh.

Rarez, at that loc I noticed that usually a Wurm or Wasp will spawn, occaisonally a Gorilla. It's kinda in the trees a little, but you can pull to the hill nearby fairly safely. They will wander so best bet is to kill all the Wurms in the area, when she finally spawned she was the 51 variety so we had to give her a pass.
▲ Page top
Phurzikon
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2001 at 4:09 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751633705
4 posts
Phurzikon, I think I have the name spelled right. Dropped this for us the other night. I was told Bard Epic guts dropped from him, so we tried him out. Killed one of us the first try, and the second we got him and he gated, but I tracked him down and a Add got our warrior as we were manaless at the end of the fight. Basically we were a group of lvl 45 to 48, one druid for heals, wiz, mage, bard (me), war & a monk. Gave the monk the coat. Phurzikon con'd white to me at 45.

Elanna, 46 bard of Innorruk.

# Dec 15 2000 at 9:42 AM Rating: Decent

Nez is the wurm for the mage FA
▲ Page top
Details Enclosed.
QuoteReply
# Dec 15 2000 at 11:28 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I have killed this Wurm 3 times while PLing my rogue. It is suppose to have the guts for the FA quest of the Mage and Bard. But out of killing it 3 times I have gotten nothign the first time, and a the Wurm Scale Coat the other 2 times. Very nice item, I plan on trading it for some very nice rogue stuff ;)

The spawn has a wandering place holder and is not timed as I killed him 2 times within the same hour last night, I was pulling everything out of the South East corner of BW all 3 times.

Nez is a 46 level Wizard Wurm with a HUGE amount of HPs (and I mean a lot here folks), max hit is for 123, and likes to cast Force Strike and Force Shock a lot. Also tries to gate when Low on Health, although I don't think it can since it is a wandering spawn.

Hope this helps.
▲ Page top
RE: Details Enclosed.
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2001 at 6:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750565886
36 posts
well after reading this post i decided to get get one of these babys for my monk, killed him 2 times, both times he was 51, so look out if your looking for a 46 wurm as the post above implys
▲ Page top
RE: Details Enclosed.
QuoteReply
# Dec 28 2000 at 5:35 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
He can gate and he can summon if he spawns high enough (very first time I fought him he did both). We dropped him 5 times tonight - cloak twice, zip the other 3. No embers at all...
▲ Page top
Real Deal
QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2001 at 4:27 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Heres the real scoop on the named wurms:

I dont have the loc with me but they spawn at a certain point in BW around n600 n2250 or something like that. They have placeholders and once they are killed they will respawn every 75seconds, Yes every 75 seconds. They will remain at the spawn point for 1 minute before starting to wander all over the SE corner of the zone. The trick is to find the PH and then sit with your group at the spawn point and start to kill. In about 10 hours of camping the spot we received the mage burning embers 2 times, the cloak 2 times, and the bard guts almost every other time. Hope this helps

Myddraal
Solusek Ro
Bloodstone Templars
▲ Page top
RE: Real Deal
QuoteReply
# May 12 2001 at 12:52 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Myddraal, thanks for all the information, but do you still have the exact loc? I spent 6-7 hours around that area killing random mobs, but none of the named wurms spawned. So, I was never able to find the PH. Any more information you have would be a great help.

Thanks
Rarez
E'ci
